message:
fix(tools): isolate spawned processes into dedicated process groups for reliable cleanup

Previously, spawned child processes inherited the parent's process group,
which meant kill_process_tree() could miss orphaned grandchildren or
accidentally signal sibling processes. This change:

- Adds isolate_command_process_group(&mut Command) which calls
  pre_exec(|| { libc::setpgid(0, 0); }) to put each child into its
  own process group before exec
- Applies it to all spawn sites: extension_dispatcher.rs (tool exec
  and MCP stdio), extensions.rs (hostcall exec_ref), extensions_js.rs
  (JS runtime child process), interactive/share.rs (share command),
  and rpc.rs (bash execution)
- Renames kill_process_tree → kill_process_group_tree at all call sites
  to use the PGID-aware variant that sends signals to the entire group
- tools.rs: formatting cleanup on ProcessGuard::new and
  kill_process_tree_with signatures; reformats UTF-8 error string

Test coverage:
- tests/e2e_rpc.rs: new rpc_abort_bash_kills_background_children test
  (Linux-only) that spawns a bash command with a background child,
  aborts via RPC, waits 4 seconds, and verifies the background child
  did not survive to write its marker file

Also adds OpenAI provider improvements:
- providers/openai.rs: authorization_override() helper that checks
  StreamOptions headers then compat custom_headers for an Authorization
  value, replacing the previous has_authorization_header boolean check;
  new test compat_authorization_header_is_used_without_api_key

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
